NKJV.1: If then you were raised with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ is, sitting at the right hand of God.
NKJV.2: Set your mind on things above, not on things on the earth.
NKJV.3: For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
NKJV.4: When Christ who is our life appears, then you also will appear with Him in glory.
NKJV.5: Therefore put to death your members which are on the earth: fornication, uncleanness, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.
NKJV.6: Because of these things the wrath of God is coming upon the sons of disobedience,
NKJV.7: in which you yourselves once walked when you lived in them.
NKJV.8: But now you yourselves are to put off all these: anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy language out of your mouth.
NKJV.9: Do not lie to one another, since you have put off the old man with his deeds,
NKJV.10: and have put on the new man who is renewed in knowledge according to the image of Him who created him,
NKJV.11: where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcised nor uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave nor free, but Christ is all and in all.
NKJV.12: Therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, put on tender mercies, kindness, humility, meekness, longsuffering;
NKJV.13: bearing with one another, and forgiving one another, if anyone has a complaint against another; even as Christ forgave you, so you also must do.
NKJV.14: But above all these things put on love, which is the bond of perfection.
NKJV.15: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which also you were called in one body; and be thankful.
NKJV.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom, teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.
NKJV.17: And whatever you do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through Him.
NKJV.18: Wives, submit to your own husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
NKJV.19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be bitter toward them.
NKJV.20: Children, obey your parents in all things, for this is well pleasing to the Lord.
NKJV.21: Fathers, do not provoke your children, lest they become discouraged.
NKJV.22: Bondservants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh, not with eyeservice, as men-pleasers, but in sincerity of heart, fearing God.
NKJV.23: And whatever you do, do it heartily, as to the Lord and not to men,
NKJV.24: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.
NKJV.25: But he who does wrong will be repaid for what he has done, and there is no partiality.

ESV.1: If then you have been raised with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
ESV.2: Set your minds on things that are above, not on things that are on earth.
ESV.3: For you have died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
ESV.4: When Christ who is your  life appears, then you also will appear with him in glory.
ESV.5: Put to death therefore what is earthly in you:  sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.
ESV.6: On account of these the wrath of God is coming.
ESV.7: In these you too once walked, when you were living in them.
ESV.8: But now you must put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and obscene talk from your mouth.
ESV.9: Do not lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old self  with its practices
ESV.10: and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge after the image of its creator.
ESV.11: Here there is not Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave,  free; but Christ is all, and in all.
ESV.12: Put on then, as God's chosen ones, holy and beloved, compassionate hearts, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience,
ESV.13: bearing with one another and, if one has a complaint against another, forgiving each other; as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.
ESV.14: And above all these put on love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony.
ESV.15: And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in one body. And be thankful.
ESV.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom, singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, with thankfulness in your hearts to God.
ESV.17: And whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
ESV.18: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
ESV.19: Husbands, love your wives, and do not be harsh with them.
ESV.20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this pleases the Lord.
ESV.21: Fathers, do not provoke your children, lest they become discouraged.
ESV.22: Bondservants, obey in everything those who are your earthly masters,  not by way of eye-service, as people-pleasers, but with sincerity of heart, fearing the Lord.
ESV.23: Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men,
ESV.24: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward. You are serving the Lord Christ.
ESV.25: For the wrongdoer will be paid back for the wrong he has done, and there is no partiality.

NASB.1: Therefore if you have been raised up with Christ, keep seeking the things above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
NASB.2: Set your mind on the things above, not on the things that are on earth.
NASB.3: For you have died and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
NASB.4: When Christ, who is our life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with Him in glory.
NASB.5: Therefore consider the members of your earthly body as dead to immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and greed, which amounts to idolatry.
NASB.6: For it is because of these things that the wrath of God will come upon the sons of disobedience,
NASB.7: and in them you also once walked, when you were living in them.
NASB.8: But now you also, put them all aside: anger, wrath, malice, slander, [and] abusive speech from your mouth.
NASB.9: Do not lie to one another, since you laid aside the old self with its [evil] practices,
NASB.10: and have put on the new self who is being renewed to a true knowledge according to the image of the One who created him--
NASB.11: [a renewal] in which there is no [distinction between] Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave and freeman, but Christ is all, and in all.
NASB.12: So, as those who have been chosen of God, holy and beloved, put on a heart of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience;
NASB.13: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, whoever has a complaint against anyone; just as the Lord forgave you, so also should you.
NASB.14: Beyond all these things [put on] love, which is the perfect bond of unity.
NASB.15: Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in one body; and be thankful.
NASB.16: Let the word of Christ richly dwell within you, with all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms [and] hymns [and] spiritual songs, singing with thankfulness in your hearts to God.
NASB.17: Whatever you do in word or deed, [do] all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks through Him to God the Father.
NASB.18: Wives, be subject to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
NASB.19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be embittered against them.
NASB.20: Children, be obedient to your parents in all things, for this is well-pleasing to the Lord.
NASB.21: Fathers, do not exasperate your children, so that they will not lose heart.
NASB.22: Slaves, in all things obey those who are your masters on earth, not with external service, as those who [merely] please men, but with sincerity of heart, fearing the Lord.
NASB.23: Whatever you do, do your work heartily, as for the Lord rather than for men,
NASB.24: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance. It is the Lord Christ whom you serve.
NASB.25: For he who does wrong will receive the consequences of the wrong which he has done, and that without partiality.

NLT.1: Since you have been raised to new life with Christ, set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God’s right hand.
NLT.2: Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth.
NLT.3: For you died to this life, and your real life is hidden with Christ in God.
NLT.4: And when Christ, who is your life, is revealed to the whole world, you will share in all his glory.
NLT.5: So put to death the sinful, earthly things lurking within you. Have nothing to do with sexual immorality, impurity, lust, and evil desires. Don’t be greedy, for a greedy person is an idolater, worshiping the things of this world.
NLT.6: Because of these sins, the anger of God is coming.
NLT.7: You used to do these things when your life was still part of this world.
NLT.8: But now is the time to get rid of anger, rage, malicious behavior, slander, and dirty language.
NLT.9: Don’t lie to each other, for you have stripped off your old sinful nature and all its wicked deeds.
NLT.10: Put on your new nature, and be renewed as you learn to know your Creator and become like him.
NLT.11: In this new life, it doesn’t matter if you are a Jew or a Gentile, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbaric, uncivilized, slave, or free. Christ is all that matters, and he lives in all of us.
NLT.12: Since God chose you to be the holy people he loves, you must clothe yourselves with tenderhearted mercy, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience.
NLT.13: Make allowance for each other’s faults, and forgive anyone who offends you. Remember, the Lord forgave you, so you must forgive others.
NLT.14: Above all, clothe yourselves with love, which binds us all together in perfect harmony.
NLT.15: And let the peace that comes from Christ rule in your hearts. For as members of one body you are called to live in peace. And always be thankful.
NLT.16: Let the message about Christ, in all its richness, fill your lives. Teach and counsel each other with all the wisdom he gives. Sing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs to God with thankful hearts.
NLT.17: And whatever you do or say, do it as a representative of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks through him to God the Father.
NLT.18: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting for those who belong to the Lord.
NLT.19: Husbands, love your wives and never treat them harshly.
NLT.20: Children, always obey your parents, for this pleases the Lord.
NLT.21: Fathers, do not aggravate your children, or they will become discouraged.
NLT.22: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything you do. Try to please them all the time, not just when they are watching you. Serve them sincerely because of your reverent fear of the Lord.
NLT.23: Work willingly at whatever you do, as though you were working for the Lord rather than for people.
NLT.24: Remember that the Lord will give you an inheritance as your reward, and that the Master you are serving is Christ.
NLT.25: But if you do what is wrong, you will be paid back for the wrong you have done. For God has no favorites.

WEB.1: If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God.
WEB.2: Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth.
WEB.3: For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
WEB.4: When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you will also be revealed with him in glory.
WEB.5: Put to death therefore your members which are on the earth: sexual immorality, uncleanness, depraved passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry;
WEB.6: for which things’ sake the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience.
WEB.7: You also once walked in those, when you lived in them;
WEB.8: but now you also put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and shameful speaking out of your mouth.
WEB.9: Don’t lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old man with his doings,
WEB.10: and have put on the new man, who is being renewed in knowledge after the image of his Creator,
WEB.11: where there can’t be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, bondservant, freeman; but Christ is all, and in all.
WEB.12: Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance;
WEB.13: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.
WEB.14: Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection.
WEB.15: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which also you were called in one body; and be thankful.
WEB.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your heart to the Lord.
WEB.17: Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him.
WEB.18: Wives, be in subjection to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
WEB.19: Husbands, love your wives, and don’t be bitter against them.
WEB.20: Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord.
WEB.21: Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.
WEB.22: Servants, obey in all things those who are your masters according to the flesh, not just when they are looking, as men pleasers, but in singleness of heart, fearing God.
WEB.23: And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord, and not for men,
WEB.24: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.
WEB.25: But he who does wrong will receive again for the wrong that he has done, and there is no partiality.

CSB17.1: So if you have been raised with Christ, seek the things above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.2: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.3: For you died, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
CSB17.4: When Christ, who is your <sup>[1]</sup> life, appears, <sup>ⓑ</sup> then you also will appear with him in glory. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.5: Therefore, put to death what belongs to your earthly nature: sexual immorality, impurity, <sup>ⓓ</sup> lust, <sup>ⓔ</sup> evil desire, and greed, <sup>ⓕ</sup> which is idolatry. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.6: Because of these, God’s wrath <sup>ⓗ</sup> is coming upon the disobedient, <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.7: and you once walked in these things when you were living <sup>ⓘ</sup> in them.
CSB17.8: But now, put away <sup>ⓙ</sup> all the following: anger, <sup>ⓚ</sup> wrath, <sup>ⓛ</sup> malice, <sup>ⓜ</sup> slander, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and filthy language from your mouth. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: Do not lie <sup>ⓑ</sup> to one another, since you have put off <sup>ⓒ</sup> the old self <sup>ⓓ</sup> with its practices
CSB17.10: and have put on <sup>ⓔ</sup> the new self. You are being renewed in knowledge according to the image of your <sup>[3]</sup> Creator. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.11: In Christ there is not Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, slave and free; but Christ is all and in all. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.12: Therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and dearly loved, <sup>ⓗ</sup> put on compassion, kindness, <sup>ⓘ</sup> humility, <sup>ⓙ</sup> gentleness, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and patience, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.13: bearing with one another and forgiving one another if anyone has a grievance against another. Just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you are also to forgive. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.14: Above all, put on love, which is the perfect bond of unity.
CSB17.15: And let the peace of Christ, to which you were also called <sup>ⓝ</sup> in one body, rule your hearts. And be thankful.
CSB17.16: Let the word of Christ dwell richly among you, in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another through psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, <sup>[4]</sup> singing to God with gratitude in your hearts.
CSB17.17: And whatever you do, in word or in deed, do everything in the name <sup>ⓐ</sup> of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
CSB17.18: Wives, submit yourselves to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.19: Husbands, <sup>ⓒ</sup> love your wives <sup>ⓓ</sup> and don’t be bitter <sup>ⓔ</sup> toward them.
CSB17.20: Children, <sup>ⓕ</sup> obey your parents in everything, for this pleases the Lord.
CSB17.21: Fathers, <sup>ⓖ</sup> do not exasperate <sup>ⓗ</sup> your children, so that they won’t become discouraged.
CSB17.22: Slaves, obey your human <sup>ⓘ</sup> masters in everything. Don’t work only while being watched, as people-pleasers, but work wholeheartedly, fearing the Lord. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.23: Whatever you do, do it from the heart, as something done for the Lord and not for people, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.24: knowing that you will receive the reward of an inheritance from the Lord. You serve the Lord Christ.
CSB17.25: For the wrongdoer <sup>ⓛ</sup> will be paid back for whatever wrong he has done, and there is no favoritism.

AMP.1: Therefore if you have been raised with Christ [to a new life, sharing in His resurrection from the dead], keep seeking the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
AMP.2: Set your mind and keep focused habitually on the things above [the heavenly things], not on things that are on the earth [which have only temporal value].
AMP.3: For you died [to this world], and your [new, real] life is hidden with Christ in God.
AMP.4: When Christ, who is our life, appears, then you also will appear with Him in glory.
AMP.5: So put to death and deprive of power the evil longings of your earthly body [with its sensual, self-centered instincts] immorality, impurity, sinful passion, evil desire, and greed, which is [a kind of] idolatry [because it replaces your devotion to God].
AMP.6: Because of these [sinful] things the [divine] wrath of God is coming on the sons of disobedience [those who fail to listen and who routinely and obstinately disregard God's precepts],
AMP.7: and in these [sinful things] you also once walked, when you were habitually living in them [without the knowledge of Christ].
AMP.8: But now rid yourselves [completely] of all these things: anger, rage, malice, slander, and obscene (abusive, filthy, vulgar) language from your mouth.
AMP.9: Do not lie to one another, for you have stripped off the old self with its evil practices,
AMP.10: and have put on the new [spiritual] self who is being continually renewed in true knowledge in the image of Him who created the new self—
AMP.11: a renewal in which there is no [distinction between] Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, [nor between nations whether] barbarian or Scythian, [nor in status whether] slave or free, but Christ is all, and in all [so believers are equal in Christ, without distinction].
AMP.12: So, as God's own chosen people, who are holy [set apart, sanctified for His purpose] and well-beloved [by God Himself], put on a heart of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience [which has the power to endure whatever injustice or unpleasantness comes, with good temper];
AMP.13: bearing graciously with one another, and willingly forgiving each other if one has a cause for complaint against another; just as the Lord has forgiven you, so should you forgive.
AMP.14: Beyond all these things put on and wrap yourselves in [unselfish] love, which is the perfect bond of unity [for everything is bound together in agreement when each one seeks the best for others].
AMP.15: Let the peace of Christ [the inner calm of one who walks daily with Him] be the controlling factor in your hearts [deciding and settling questions that arise]. To this peace indeed you were called as members in one body [of believers]. And be thankful [to God always].
AMP.16: Let the [spoken] word of Christ have its home within you [dwelling in your heart and mind—permeating every aspect of your being] as you teach [spiritual things] and admonish and train one another with all wisdom, singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs with thankfulness in your hearts to God.
AMP.17: Whatever you do [no matter what it is] in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus [and in dependence on Him], giving thanks to God the Father through Him.
AMP.18: Wives, be subject to your husbands [out of respect for their position as protector, and their accountability to God], as is proper and fitting in the Lord.
AMP.19: Husbands, love your wives [with an affectionate, sympathetic, selfless love that always seeks the best for them] and do not be embittered or resentful toward them [because of the responsibilities of marriage].
AMP.20: Children, obey your parents [as God's representatives] in all things, for this [attitude of respect and obedience] is well-pleasing to the Lord [and will bring you God's promised blessings].
AMP.21: Fathers, do not provoke or irritate or exasperate your children [with demands that are trivial or unreasonable or humiliating or abusive; nor by favoritism or indifference; treat them tenderly with lovingkindness], so they will not lose heart and become discouraged or unmotivated [with their spirits broken].
AMP.22: Servants, in everything obey those who are your masters on earth, not only with external service, as those who merely please people, but with sincerity of heart because of your fear of the Lord.
AMP.23: Whatever you do [whatever your task may be], work from the soul [that is, put in your very best effort], as [something done] for the Lord and not for men,
AMP.24: knowing [with all certainty] that it is from the Lord [not from men] that you will receive the inheritance which is your [greatest] reward. It is the Lord Christ whom you [actually] serve.
AMP.25: For he who does wrong will be punished for his wrongdoing, and [with God] there is no partiality [no special treatment based on a person's position in life].

MSG.1: So if you're serious about living this new resurrection life with Christ, act like it. Pursue the things over which Christ presides.
MSG.2: Don't shuffle along, eyes to the ground, absorbed with the things right in front of you. Look up, and be alert to what is going on around Christ—that's where the action is. See things from his perspective.
MSG.3: Your old life is dead. Your new life, which is your real life—even though invisible to spectators—is with Christ in God. He is your life.
MSG.4: When Christ (your real life, remember) shows up again on this earth, you'll show up, too—the real you, the glorious you. Meanwhile, be content with obscurity, like Christ.
MSG.5: And that means killing off everything connected with that way of death: sexual promiscuity, impurity, lust, doing whatever you feel like whenever you feel like it, and grabbing whatever attracts your fancy. That's a life shaped by things and feelings instead of by God.
MSG.6: It's because of this kind of thing that God is about to explode in anger.
MSG.7: It wasn't long ago that you were doing all that stuff and not knowing any better.
MSG.8: But you know better now, so make sure it's all gone for good: bad temper, irritability, meanness, profanity, dirty talk.
MSG.9: Don't lie to one another. You're done with that old life. It's like a filthy set of ill-fitting clothes you've stripped off and put in the fire.
MSG.10: Now you're dressed in a new wardrobe. Every item of your new way of life is custom-made by the Creator, with his label on it. All the old fashions are now obsolete.
MSG.11: Words like Jewish and non-Jewish, religious and irreligious, insider and outsider, uncivilized and uncouth, slave and free, mean nothing. From now on everyone is defined by Christ, everyone is included in Christ.
MSG.12: So, chosen by God for this new life of love, dress in the wardrobe God picked out for you: compassion, kindness, humility, quiet strength, discipline.
MSG.13: Be even-tempered, content with second place, quick to forgive an offense. Forgive as quickly and completely as the Master forgave you.
MSG.14: And regardless of what else you put on, wear love. It's your basic, all-purpose garment. Never be without it.
MSG.15: Let the peace of Christ keep you in tune with each other, in step with each other. None of this going off and doing your own thing. And cultivate thankfulness.
MSG.16: Let the Word of Christ—the Message—have the run of the house. Give it plenty of room in your lives. Instruct and direct one another using good common sense. And sing, sing your hearts out to God!
MSG.17: Let every detail in your lives—words, actions, whatever—be done in the name of the Master, Jesus, thanking God the Father every step of the way.
MSG.18: Wives, understand and support your husbands by submitting to them in ways that honor the Master.
MSG.19: Husbands, go all out in love for your wives. Don't take advantage of them.
MSG.20: Children, do what your parents tell you. This delights the Master no end.
MSG.21: Parents, don't come down too hard on your children or you'll crush their spirits.
MSG.22: Servants, do what you're told by your earthly masters. And don't just do the minimum that will get you by. Do your best.
MSG.23: Work from the heart for your real Master, for God,
MSG.24: confident that you'll get paid in full when you come into your inheritance. Keep in mind always that the ultimate Master you're serving is Christ.
MSG.25: The sullen servant who does shoddy work will be held responsible. Being Christian doesn't cover up bad work.

NIV.1:
Rules for Holy Living
Since, then, you have been raised with Christ, set your hearts on things above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God.
NIV.2: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.
NIV.3: For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.
NIV.4: When Christ, who is your life, appears, then you also will appear with him in glory.
NIV.5: Put to death, therefore, whatever belongs to your earthly nature: sexual immorality, impurity, lust, evil desires and greed, which is idolatry.
NIV.6: Because of these, the wrath of God is coming.
NIV.7: You used to walk in these ways, in the life you once lived.
NIV.8: But now you must rid yourselves of all such things as these: anger, rage, malice, slander, and filthy language from your lips.
NIV.9: Do not lie to each other, since you have taken off your old self with its practices
NIV.10: and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge in the image of its Creator.
NIV.11: Here there is no Greek or Jew, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave or free, but Christ is all, and is in all.
NIV.12: Therefore, as God’s chosen people, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience.
NIV.13: Bear with each other and forgive whatever grievances you may have against one another. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
NIV.14: And over all these virtues put on love, which binds them all together in perfect unity.
NIV.15: Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, since as members of one body you were called to peace. And be thankful.
NIV.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly as you teach and admonish one another with all wisdom, and as you sing psalms, hymns and spiritual songs with gratitude in your hearts to God.
NIV.17: And whatever you do, whether in word or deed, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
NIV.18:
Rules for Christian Households
Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
NIV.19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.
NIV.20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this pleases the Lord.
NIV.21: Fathers, do not embitter your children, or they will become discouraged.
NIV.22: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything; and do it, not only when their eye is on you and to win their favor, but with sincerity of heart and reverence for the Lord.
NIV.23: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for men,
NIV.24: since you know that you will receive an inheritance from the Lord as a reward. It is the Lord Christ you are serving.
NIV.25: Anyone who does wrong will be repaid for his wrong, and there is no favoritism.

NRSVCE.1: So if you have been raised with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
NRSVCE.2: Set your minds on things that are above, not on things that are on earth,
NRSVCE.3: for you have died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
NRSVCE.4: When Christ who is your life is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.
NRSVCE.5: Put to death, therefore, whatever in you is earthly: fornication, impurity, passion, evil desire, and greed (which is idolatry).
NRSVCE.6: On account of these the wrath of God is coming on those who are disobedient.
NRSVCE.7: These are the ways you also once followed, when you were living that life.
NRSVCE.8: But now you must get rid of all such things — anger, wrath, malice, slander, and abusive language from your mouth.
NRSVCE.9: Do not lie to one another, seeing that you have stripped off the old self with its practices
NRSVCE.10: and have clothed yourselves with the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of its creator.
NRSVCE.11: In that renewal there is no longer Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave and free; but Christ is all and in all!
NRSVCE.12: As God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience.
NRSVCE.13: Bear with one another and, if anyone has a complaint against another, forgive each other; just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.
NRSVCE.14: Above all, clothe yourselves with love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony.
NRSVCE.15: And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in the one body. And be thankful.
NRSVCE.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; teach and admonish one another in all wisdom; and with gratitude in your hearts sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs to God.
NRSVCE.17: And whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
NRSVCE.18: Wives, be subject to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
NRSVCE.19: Husbands, love your wives and never treat them harshly.
NRSVCE.20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is your acceptable duty in the Lord.
NRSVCE.21: Fathers, do not provoke your children, or they may lose heart.
NRSVCE.22: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything, not only while being watched and in order to please them, but wholeheartedly, fearing the Lord.
NRSVCE.23: Whatever your task, put yourselves into it, as done for the Lord and not for your masters,
NRSVCE.24: since you know that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward; you serve the Lord Christ.
NRSVCE.25: For the wrongdoer will be paid back for whatever wrong has been done, and there is no partiality.

NET.1: Therefore, if you have been raised with Christ, keep seeking the things above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
NET.2: Keep thinking about things above, not things on the earth,
NET.3: for you have died and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
NET.4: When Christ (who is your life) appears, then you too will be revealed in glory with him.
NET.5: So put to death whatever in your nature belongs to the earth: sexual immorality, impurity, shameful passion, evil desire, and greed which is idolatry.
NET.6: Because of these things the wrath of God is coming on the sons of disobedience.
NET.7: You also lived your lives in this way at one time, when you used to live among them.
NET.8: But now, put off all such things as anger, rage, malice, slander, abusive language from your mouth.
NET.9: Do not lie to one another since you have put off the old man with its practices
NET.10: and have been clothed with the new man that is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of the one who created it.
NET.11: Here there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave or free, but Christ is all and in all.
NET.12: Therefore, as the elect of God, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with a heart of mercy, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience,
NET.13: bearing with one another and forgiving one another, if someone happens to have a complaint against anyone else. Just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also forgive others.
NET.14: And to all these virtues add love, which is the perfect bond.
NET.15: Let the peace of Christ be in control in your heart (for you were in fact called as one body to this peace), and be thankful.
NET.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, teaching and exhorting one another with all wisdom, singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, all with grace in your hearts to God.
NET.17: And whatever you do in word or deed, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
NET.18: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
NET.19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be embittered against them.
NET.20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is pleasing in the Lord.
NET.21: Fathers, do not provoke your children, so they will not become disheartened.
NET.22: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in every respect, not only when they are watching — like those who are strictly people-pleasers — but with a sincere heart, fearing the Lord.
NET.23: Whatever you are doing, work at it with enthusiasm, as to the Lord and not for people,
NET.24: because you know that you will receive your inheritance from the Lord as the reward. Serve the Lord Christ.
NET.25: For the one who does wrong will be repaid for his wrong, and there are no exceptions.
